mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion and consumer income growth, which drives discretionary spending and equity valuations upward over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2009 recovery phase with employment gains accelerating
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows month-over-month growth below 0.3%, the real interest rate is deeply negative, incentivizing equity allocation over cash and bonds, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Late 2010 environment with near-zero rates and subdued inflation
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with low inflation expectations
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of 60-day forward returns, or the mechanism fails to outperform single-series FEDFUNDS model by Rank IC > 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 2% signals weakening consumer demand and future earnings pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales volatility in 2010 recovery phase
regime_hint: demand-sensitive regime with high earnings revision risk
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above its 3-month moving average, signaling improving capacity utilization and pricing power, which supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or the signal shows negative correlation with forward returns in any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both accelerate month-over-month simultaneously, indicating broad-based demand expansion across residential and consumer sectors, driving S&P 500 returns positive over 60 trading days through multiplier effects on corporate earnings.
candidate_trigger: HOUST monthly growth > 3% AND RSAFS monthly growth > 1.5%
regime_hint: synchronized demand recovery across housing and consumption
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10) in 60-day forward return regression, or joint signal underperforms either single-series model by Rank IC > 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, signaling labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.8% in November 2010, elevated from crisis lows
regime_hint: labor market weakness or deterioration phase
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 54% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) is below 2.5% annualized and the federal funds rate is above 1.5%, the Fed faces policy constraints that limit further accommodation, signaling a tightening bias that compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-crisis recovery with subdued growth trajectory
regime_hint: low-growth regime with policy constraint risk
failure_condition: Interaction term is not significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression, or positive 60-day returns exceed 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 1%, signaling supply-side contraction and potential demand weakness, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices recession risk.
regime_hint: supply-side contraction or demand shock regime
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 53% of triggered instances, or the signal shows positive correlation with forward returns in any 12-month rolling window.
